hints: Nouns for how smoke or vapor gathers in the air—from a drifting sheet to a tight little wisp you can almost cup in your hand.
Shoreline tackle talk: lure, snag, mesh, and the long stick you flick over the water—same hobby, four staples.
Think one notorious spider in headlines: sticky architecture, a toxin, a body-shape clue, and a grim mating-table rumor.
Each word begins a familiar two-word catchphrase whose second half is always the same calendar day—culture, commerce, music, and food all line up.
